title: A Nordic-inspired sauna just opened in the White Mountains
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: White Mountain Sauna Haus opened in North Conway, N.H., featuring two Finnish saunas, plunge pools, and a cafe.
The facility offers communal sauna experiences with health benefits, attracting visitors from New England and globally.
Open Monday, Thursday, Friday noon to 9 p.m., Saturday and Sunday 10 a.m. to 9 p.m.; single visit costs $36.

title: What Patriots coach Mike Vrabel said about viral photos with NFL reporter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel addressed viral photos with NFL reporter Dianna Russini, calling the interactions 'completely innocent.'
The photos show Vrabel and Russini in a hot tub and holding hands at a Sedona, Arizona resort, sparking speculation.
Dianna Russini and The Athletic's Steven Ginsburg stated the photos were misleading and lacked context, reflecting only part of a group gathering.

title: The perfect spring weekend starts with daffodils
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: New England fields of daffodils offer picturesque spring weekend activities across Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and Connecticut.
The historic Lowell church plans to become the Jack Kerouac Center, featuring a museum and other facilities.
JetBlue raises checked baggage fees by $4 off-peak and $9 peak for economy passengers.

title: Matt Damon and Noah Kahan will join forces on ‘SNL’ in May
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon will host 'Saturday Night Live' on May 9, 2026, marking his third time in the role.
Noah Kahan will be the musical guest on 'SNL' for the second time, promoting his album 'The Great Divide.'
Olivia Rodrigo, Matt Damon, Noah Kahan, and Will Ferrell set for 'SNL's' final episodes of its 51st season.

title: Land court backs Somerville church’s plan for homeless shelter that raised neighborhood concerns
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A Land Court judge ruled that First Church Somerville can open a 26-bed homeless shelter using the Dover Amendment.
The Dover Amendment allows religious institutions to bypass local zoning for projects serving a religious purpose, like the shelter.
Neighbors expressed concerns over the shelter's 'low-barrier' status, fearing increased drug activity in the community.

title: Everett moves to rein in data centers as pushback grows across Massachusetts
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Everett's Planning Board endorsed a zoning ordinance to restrict data centers in the Docklands Innovation District.
Data centers in Everett's EDID would need special permits and be limited to 20,000 square feet or 5 megawatts.
The proposal is intended as a proactive measure amid rising concerns about data center impacts in Massachusetts.

title: South Shore med spa owner pleads guilty to administering thousands of fake Botox and other treatments
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Stoughton med spa owner Rebecca Fadanelli pleaded guilty to charges related to administering counterfeit cosmetic injections.
Fadanelli admitted to importing and using fake Botox from China and Brazil, receiving over $1 million from clients.
Authorities seized counterfeit products shipped from China, and client complaints led to investigation by the FDA.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about praise from Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla, Boston Celtics coach, credits players for success after Giannis Antetokounmpo praised his leadership mindset.
Celtics maintained competitiveness despite roster changes, securing the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs.
Brad Stevens highlights strong staff chemistry as key to Celtics' ability to adjust and remain successful this season.

title: ‘That tells me a lot’: Alex Cora impressed by Trevor Story’s response to early-season adversity
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Trevor Story broke out of early-season slump, contributing crucial hits in recent games for the Boston Red Sox.
Manager Alex Cora praised Story's determination after he insisted on playing despite initial lineup uncertainties.
Story's performance improvement mirrors a similar early-season turnaround from 2025, offering hope for the team.

title: Swansea police recover 5 guns from home after report that second grader brought weapon to school
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Swansea police seized five unsecured, unregistered guns from a home after a second grader allegedly brought a gun to school.
Police and K-9 units found no weapons at Mark G. Hoyle Elementary School, but increased safety measures are in place.
Weapons-related charges are planned against two individuals, names withheld, following search and recovery of firearms.
